message:
fix(e2e): prevent recursive log writes during teardown log dump

Fix a subtle issue where reading TEST_LOG_FILE during teardown could
trigger additional log writes (via test_debug), causing infinite
recursion or corrupted log output.

common.bash:
- Save and clear TEST_LOG_FILE and TEST_LOG_FILE_SECONDARY before
  reading the log contents in teardown_test_env
- Restore the variables after the read loop completes
- Prevents test_debug calls inside the loop from appending to the file
  being read

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -200,10 +200,21 @@ teardown_test_env() {
 
     # Log any test artifacts before cleanup
     if [[ -f "$TEST_LOG_FILE" ]]; then
+        # Avoid recursive writes while reading the log file.
+        local saved_log_file="$TEST_LOG_FILE"
+        local saved_log_file_secondary="${TEST_LOG_FILE_SECONDARY:-}"
+        TEST_LOG_FILE=""
+        TEST_LOG_FILE_SECONDARY=""
+
         test_debug "Test log contents:"
         while read -r line; do
             test_debug "  $line"
-        done < "$TEST_LOG_FILE"
+        done < "$saved_log_file"
+
+        TEST_LOG_FILE="$saved_log_file"
+        if [[ -n "$saved_log_file_secondary" ]]; then
+            TEST_LOG_FILE_SECONDARY="$saved_log_file_secondary"
+        fi
     fi
 
     # Intentionally do not delete any files/directories here.
